title: iterable is not async iterable‚Äá
author: ksmarty
body:
I'm trying to read an xlsx file, but every time I get the error `iterable is not async iterable`.

I've tried reading a file written by exceljs and one created externally but both report the same error. I also attempted reading the file with `fs` and passing the buffer to exceljs but got the same error. Here is my code:

```ts
import Excel from "exceljs";
const workbook = new Excel.Workbook();
await workbook.xlsx.readFile("very_cool_file.xlsx");
```

And here is the full error:
```
iterable is not async iterable‚Äá
  ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčat ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčmodule.exports‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč./node_modules/exceljs/lib/utils/parse-sax.js:20‚Äč
  ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčat ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčRelationshipsXform.parse‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč./node_modules/exceljs/lib/xlsx/xform/base-xform.js:60‚Äč
  ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčat ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčRelationshipsXform.parseStream‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč./node_modules/exceljs/lib/xlsx/xform/base-xform.js:77‚Äč
  ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčat ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčXLSX.parseRels‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč./node_modules/exceljs/lib/xlsx/xlsx.js:66‚Äč
  ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčat ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčXLSX.load‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč./node_modules/exceljs/lib/xlsx/xlsx.js:316‚Äč
  ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčat ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčasync XLSX.readFile‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč./node_modules/exceljs/lib/xlsx/xlsx.js:55
```

node: v16.9.1
TypeScript: v4.4.2

comments.author: ksmarty
comments.body: I ultimately gave up and used python with xlwings. Much better tool, honestly.

comments.author: RedEyePilot
comments.body:
> same problem here

Were you able to find a fix? Same problem here.

upd (resolved):
So after some poking around figured out that there is interference with package readable-stream which in my case is a dependency of mysql package. Once it is removed (anyways mysql does not look supported any more), the error goes away. I do not have enough competency to figure out what exactly is wrong inside either of those, so delete and forget.

Once it is removed (anyways mysql does not look supported any more), the error goes away. I do not have enough competency to figure out what exactly is wrong inside either of those, so delete and forget.

Hello, same problem here. What do you mean removed? readable-stream is also a dependency of exceljs, right?

comments.author: RedEyePilot
comments.body:
> > Were you able to find a fix? Same problem here.

So after poking around some more I came to the conclusion that some of the dependencies were missing. Deleting readable-stream (even though it is a dependency of exceljs) did resolve the issue without affecting the functionality I needed. However the real cause, in my mind, was that more dependencies were missing. I ran inventory and realized that somewhere on the way I lost a number of other dependencies. I ended up reinstalling the packages I use to a clean project and replaced the node_modules folder in the old one with this new one. This time readable-stream was there and the error did not reappear. So that could be the case.
